title: centaurea ovina subsp. caprina
intro: Centaurea ovina subsp. caprina, also known as sheep's bits, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and leafy stems. The medicinal properties of Centaurea ovina subsp. caprina have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive discomfort, making it a popular remedy in herbal medicine. Centaurea ovina subsp. caprina can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
